reasoning: The kernel achieves only 22.7% occupancy, well below the 35% threshold, meaning there are insufficient active warps to hide any latency. The kernel does essentially no computation (0.00 FLOP/byte arithmetic intensity) and barely touches memory (0.0% DRAM utilization), with no long scoreboard or memory dependency stalls — the kernel is trivially small (one shared memory write per thread) but launched with 256 threads per block while only using `threadIdx.x` up to 31 meaningfully for the 32×32 shared memory tile, resulting in minimal useful work and poor warp-level parallelism utilization. The low occupancy is the dominant factor preventing the hardware from hiding even the minimal latencies present.
